title: Phase 6b umbrella: agent cutover complete (all services)
description: Phase 6b umbrella - service-by-service cutover with per-service go/no-go (ACL both ports, >=60s retry, green differential corpus), CLAUDE.md prose deleted in the same change. Close ONLY when the '6b' completion criteria hold: every onboarded service cut over and CLAUDE.md carries only the SEAM endpoint pointer. Evidence comment required.

title: 4.1: z.ai/GLM fragment + credential + e2e injection proof
description: Phase 4. The z.ai/GLM fragment: x-vault-path under seam/routes/<svc>/ (place the credential into OpenBao under the co-owned path), x-inject-as per the upstream's auth shape, x-cost-per-call/x-quota authored in the upstream's own quota units (governor enforces at Phase 13), x-credential-probe designated safe request with cadence sized against the meter. Allowlist: the z.ai proxy host line (public host = explicit enumeration; two-PR onboarding incl. the new configMap volume rollout). This is the FIRST end-to-end injection proof against a real credential - caller copies stripped, server-side value injected, echo scrubbed. NO agent cutover (6b, behind 13).

title: 10.1: x-instance-param + map resolution + instance-segment deletion
description: Phase 10 (Data Models -> x-upstream-map, x-instance-param). Fragment-root x-instance-param (required with x-upstream-map, forbidden without - lint error + quarantine both ways; named param must exist in EVERY declared path); object-form entries {url, vaultPath, injectAs, tls, plaintext, probeInterval, breaker, requiredScope} - only url required; fragment-level fields default any omitted key EXCEPT plaintext (per-entry, never inherited; fragment-level x-upstream-plaintext beside a map is a lint error; an http:// entry without its own acknowledgement is a hard error) and requiredScope (per-entry, no default; PARSE AND CARRY only - enforcement is Phase 7's, with fragment-root x-fanout-scope parsed here too). Step 2 of the path rule: the instance segment is deleted entirely, never re-substituted; per-instance evaluation of the whole computation at stage 11 (N base URLs, one shared computed path). Every entry url bound by the upstream-host allowlist. Worked example must pass: /k8s/{cluster}/api/v1/pods + strip-prefix /k8s -> entry url + /api/v1/pods.

title: CI: seam-ci Argo WorkflowTemplate on iad-ci (Go build path)
description: ADR-001 Consequences follow-on + Testing Strategy -> CI. Argo WorkflowTemplate seam-ci in declarative-config k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/: gofmt + go vet + golangci-lint, go test -race ./..., acceptance suite, and the benchmark gate (>20% p99 regression vs committed baseline FAILS the build; meaningful from Phase 2). Pin all images by digest (never :latest). GH Actions stay disabled repo-level. The shop's cargo/cargo-remote offload does NOT apply (SEAM is Go). Blocks the Phase 1a umbrella so the same-commit DoD is enforceable from the first phase onward.

title: Add response caching + single-flight coalescing for cacheable GET routes
description:
Not yet in docs/plan/plan.md — a new improvement idea grounded in SEAM's actual topology: it will be
the single chokepoint for dozens of concurrent NEEDLE workers hitting the same read-only upstreams
(ArgoCD app list, per-cluster kubectl-proxy `get pods`, etc.). Right now every fragment's requests
pass straight through with no caching, so N workers polling the same status at the same moment means
N upstream calls.

Proposed addition: a route fragment extension `x-cache-ttl` (seconds) marking a route's response as
safe to cache; the gateway serves repeat identical requests (same route + normalized params) from an
in-memory cache within the TTL, and single-flights concurrent identical in-flight requests (only one
upstream call in-flight per (route, params) key, all concurrent callers await the same result) even
for TTL=0 (dedup only, no caching).

This is a genuine efficiency win specific to SEAM's fleet-of-callers shape, not generic advice.
Write it up as a new subsection in docs/plan/plan.md (pattern-match the existing "Per-Route Guards"
section: opt-in per route via fragment field, no SEAM-wide default) and add it as a phase or fold
into an existing one (natural fit: extends Phase 1's request-handling core). Should note the
interaction with the Credential Health Sentinel's probe traffic and with cost-governed routes
(x-cost-per-call) — a cache hit should not count against x-quota.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Fenced (status=blocked, 2026-07-22): out-of-plan backlog idea, deliberately NOT in plan.md v1.0 - not for autonomous work. Revisit only if the user promotes it into the plan.

title: Harness: differential capture + replay tool
description: Testing Strategy -> Conformance/differential harness - 'the highest-value test asset in the plan'. Build the capture + replay tooling: record real request/response pairs at an incumbent proxy into a corpus format; replay against BOTH the incumbent and SEAM; assert response-equivalence modulo the enumerated expected diffs (X-SEAM-* response headers, the injected upstream credential, bytes redacted to [REDACTED-BY-SEAM]) - a corpus entry returning byte-identically INCLUDING an echoed secret is a FAILURE. Output: a per-service corpus pass report attachable to that service's 6b cutover PR (Evidence bundle). Standalone tool; no SEAM code dependency to start - workable now.

title: 2.3: Strip-then-inject + x-inject-as + upstream path rewrite
description: Phase 2 (Architecture -> inbound header stripping + query extension; Data Models -> x-inject-as, x-upstream path computation). Stage 2: delete ALL occurrences of every injectable header (canonicalized case-insensitive per-key) and every injected query-parameter name (exact, case-sensitive), plus Authorization and all X-SEAM-* except X-SEAM-Dry-Run and X-SEAM-API-Version - for names declared by ANY matched route, not just the one in play. Injection per x-inject-as {kind: header|bearer|query, name} (bearer: name forbidden, implies Authorization: Bearer <secret>). Upstream path: steps 1/3/4 of the four-step rule - matched template never raw path, x-upstream-path-template (path-item, wins) / x-upstream-strip-prefix (fragment-root literal), re-substitution percent-encoded as exactly one segment (a binding with / or .. encoded, never expanded); base path prefix preserved; query carried as-is. Both flagship worked examples must land. Includes the seam lint path-rewrite coherence checks (template starts with /, every named param exists, no instance param; strip-prefix prefixes EVERY declared path).

title: Precondition: provision evaluator OpenBao role + GitHub token + metrics read
description: Precondition of Phase 8 (Implementation Phases -> dependency edges). For seam-retirement-evaluator: its own OpenBao Kubernetes-auth role bound to its own ServiceAccount; a dedicated GitHub token (capability: opening PRs against declarative-config - origin is GitHub, NOT Forgejo) stored at its own OpenBao path OUTSIDE seam/routes/*, readable only by the evaluator's identity and never by SEAM; read access to the VictoriaMetrics instance scraping SEAM. No SEAM dependency of its own - workable early. Done when the evaluator's identity can read its token and query the metrics store, and SEAM's role provably cannot read the token path.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Fenced from autonomous workers (status=blocked, 2026-07-22): needs OpenBao admin, a GitHub token mint, and VictoriaMetrics access grants. Operator work; unblock deliberately.

title: 6b: Cut over ArgoCD service + delete its CLAUDE.md prose
description: Phase 6b, per-service cutover: ArgoCD read-only proxy. Go/no-go checklist, ALL verified with evidence in a comment before acting: (1) ACL at BOTH ports - worker tags reach 8080 and are REFUSED on 8081; (2) this service's agents retry with backoff over >=60 s (single-replica deploys are a real 5-30 s gap); (3) its differential corpus is green on the shipped commit. Then, in ONE change: delete the ArgoCD read-only proxy prose from CLAUDE.md and point agents at SEAM's route. Blast radius = this service only; the deleted prose is git-revertible.

title: 2.2: Dual allowlists - vault-path co-ownership + upstream-host fail-closed
description: Phase 2 (Architecture). (a) Vault-path: x-vault-path must resolve inside seam/routes/<x-seam-owner>/* with co-ownership verified against the mounted parent directory; traversal, globs, templated segments rejected outright; NO secret is ever fetched for a violating fragment (quarantine first). (b) Upstream-host: operator-owned file mounted at /etc/gateway/allowlist.yaml (local dev: --allowlist-file, refused in-cluster, enumerated as allowlistSource: dev-file); suffix matches + exactly-enumerated bare hostnames, optional port constraints; IP literals rejected; ABSENT/EMPTY/UNPARSEABLE = FAIL CLOSED - no host permitted, fragments naming upstreams quarantine, readyz not-ready, /config/status names the condition. Both enforced at merge time with fragment-wide quarantine, never trusting lint to have run.

title: 6b: Cut over z.ai/GLM service + delete its CLAUDE.md prose
description: Phase 6b, per-service cutover: z.ai/GLM proxy - METERED, so additionally verify the Phase 13 governor is observably live on the route (x-quota sized deliberately, X-SEAM-Budget-Remaining observed on real responses, 402 behavior spot-checked with a test budget). Standard checklist: ACL both ports, >=60 s retry, corpus green. Then delete the z.ai proxy prose from CLAUDE.md in the same change that points agents at SEAM.

title: Phase 14 umbrella: foreign-worker ingress complete
description: Phase 14 umbrella - Cloudflare Access JWT validation at the gateway, service-token->scopes map, X-SEAM-Scopes stripping, default-deny mode. The ONLY phase that lifts the no-public-ingress rule. Close ONLY when the '14' completion criteria hold (each of the four rules has a passing named test). Evidence comment required.

title: 5.2: Nine-cluster x-upstream-map fragment + allowlist + credentials
description: Phase 5. ONE parametrized fragment at k8s/rs-manager/seam/routes/<svc>/ with x-instance-param: cluster over /k8s/{cluster}/...; nine entries - eight bare-MagicDNS urls (traefik-ardenone-cluster:8001, traefik-apexalgo-iad:8001, kubectl-proxy-iad-kalshi:8001, kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001, ...) each as its OWN line in seam-upstream-allowlist, plus rs-manager via in-cluster Service DNS (covered by *.svc.cluster.local). Per-instance credentials into OpenBao under the co-owned prefix (long-lived observer SA tokens; ~3-day OIDC admin tokens where included) with per-entry vaultPath/injectAs/probeInterval (tight cadence on 3-day tokens); DISTINCT per-instance requiredScope on admin vs observer entries (enforcement Phase 7); per-entry plaintext acknowledgements for http:// hosts. Done when a GET succeeds through SEAM against every one of the nine instances and _all returns nine labeled entries.

title: 6a.2: Tailnet ACL - per-port grants
description: Phase 6a (Pre-Phase-7 Trust Boundary). Tailnet policy file: worker tags (tag:needle-worker, tag:devpod, operator workstation source set) granted the CALLER port 8080 only; the operator source set alone granted 8081. Default-deny; tailnet membership is not the boundary, the ACL is. Verify BOTH directions - especially refusal on 8081, the item that never fails visibly when it is wrong. This grant is the 6b gate's first checklist item.

title: 2.1: OpenBao client + 30s cache + outage behavior
description: Phase 2 (Architecture -> OpenBao auth; secret caching; secret-store outage behavior, decided 2026-07-22). Kubernetes auth in-cluster; dev-token mode locally (env/flag, REFUSED when in-cluster detected via projected SA token or KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST, enumerated at /config/status as authMode: dev-token); 30 s in-memory cache keyed by resolved x-vault-path; eager 401 invalidation. Outage behavior: uncached path with OpenBao down -> structured secret-store-unavailable 503 naming OpenBao (never the upstream; breaker untouched) with Retry-After; stale NEVER served past TTL; per-vault-path fetch hold-down (default 5 s, operator knob) fails fast without new round-trips; a failed 401-refetch degrades to the same 503; /_seam/readyz unaffected mid-life. Values never logged, never on disk; internal/vault is the only package holding secret values.

title: 12.2: 401 refetch-retry-once + credential-refresh-not-retried
description: Phase 12 (Credential Health Sentinel; Scenario 4). On an upstream 401: eagerly invalidate the cached secret, refetch from OpenBao, RE-STRIP inbound headers, re-inject the fresh value, retry exactly ONCE over the Phase 2 tee (body within maxReplayableRequestBytes) - the caller sees a transparent success and /health/credentials reflects the refresh. Above the cap or unreplayable: still invalidate + refetch (next request succeeds), do NOT retry, answer with the structured credential-refresh-not-retried envelope (upstream's own status, scrubbed body, plain statement that resending now uses the refreshed credential). If the refetch itself fails, degrade to the secret-store-unavailable 503 (2.1). Charged twice on the retry path per the dispatch-time accounting rule. Scenario 4 green in both forms.

title: 8.3: x-seam-deprecated + Deprecation/Sunset + brownout scheduler
description: Phase 8 (Data Models -> x-seam-deprecated; Phase 8 -> retirement workflow channel). Object {since, sunset?, brownout?}: Deprecation emitted from the marker landing (bare true = lint error with fix hint); Sunset ONLY when author-supplied or once the quiet window opened and its projected end resolves - omitted before then, may move later never earlier; Link rel=deprecation -> /docs/route?path=&version= and /changes; per-operation deprecated: true honored. Brownout scheduler in the gateway: DORMANT on any fragment without a brownout block (fail-safe - channel failure degrades to coexistence, never to 410s); serves scheduled structured 410s naming the replacement and /changes; a caller during a brownout CANCELS retirement. Lint sanity: ordered, non-overlapping windows inside [since, sunset]; brownout requires resolved sunset and an enclosing x-seam-deprecated.

title: 6b: Cut over twitterapi.io service + delete its CLAUDE.md prose
description: Phase 6b, per-service cutover: twitterapi.io proxy - METERED (per-call credits), same additional governor verification as the z.ai cutover (credits-denominated budget observed live). Standard checklist: ACL both ports, >=60 s retry, corpus green. Then delete the twitterapi proxy prose from CLAUDE.md in the same change that points agents at SEAM.

title: 11.2: Three-state last-2xx render + /health/upstreams
description: Phase 11 (Passive Route Health). Per-route last-2xx keyed on attempts and successes SEPARATELY, three states: 'no attempt since last restart'; 'no success in N attempts since last restart' + the last error (from the breaker's tracked per-origin value - the previously invisible state an operator most needs); 'last succeeded Xm ago' with a source field (probe-labeling populated once Phase 12 traffic exists). Last-success value stays intentionally empty in the first two states. /docs renders it per path; /health/upstreams aggregates the same three states per upstream + current breaker state. In-memory, restart-scoped, and every string says so.

title: Phase 4 umbrella: metered upstreams onboarded
description: Phase 4 umbrella - z.ai/GLM + twitterapi.io fragments live; first end-to-end injection proof against real credentials; NO agent cutover (6b gate behind Phase 13). Close ONLY when the '4' completion criteria hold. Evidence comment required.

title: Phase 3 umbrella: fragments + ArgoCD pilot live
description: Phase 3 umbrella - ConfigMap-mounted fragments, hot reload with atomic swap, pass-through ArgoCD pilot serving real traffic, pilot differential corpus green. Close ONLY when the '3' completion criteria hold (sub-second zero-drop reload under load). Evidence comment required.

title: Phase 13 umbrella: guards complete
description: Phase 13 umbrella - loop breaker with canonical hash, cost governor with unit-bearing objects and dispatch-time accounting, dry-run. Forcing edge 4 + 13 -> 6b. Close ONLY when the '13' completion criteria hold (Scenarios 6 green, hash property tests green). Evidence comment required.

title: 1a.3: Control-plane branch + reserved namespace + two-listener split
description: Phase 1a (Architecture -> 'Where a control-plane request goes after stage 1'; Fragment Toolchain -> reserved namespace). Stage-1 control-plane resolution BEFORE any route table, then stage 2 (strip all X-SEAM-* bar the two exceptions), inert identity step, short-circuit before stage 4. Reserved set: the closed grandfathered enumeration + prefixes /docs/, /health/, /config/, /approvals/, /_seam/ - control-plane always wins the match. Two-listener wiring: caller 8080 (proxy + /_seam/healthz + /_seam/readyz), operator 8081 (/_seam/metrics; operator endpoints as they arrive). Done when: a forged X-SEAM-* request header demonstrably does not survive stage 2, control-plane paths resolve with no route-table consult, both listeners bound with the right endpoints on each.

title: 8.5: seam-retirement-evaluator deployable + PR opener
description: Phase 8 (Components; Hosting; Phase 8 -> evaluator paragraphs). Own Deployment at k8s/rs-manager/seam-retirement-evaluator/ (deliberately OUTSIDE seam/), own SA + OpenBao role, internal scheduling loop (fleet no-CronJobs rule). Read-only VictoriaMetrics queries: observed max inter-request gap + quiet-since per (route, x-api-version); window = max(3 x observed max gap, 7 days); insufficient history -> 7-day floor, never an invented gap. Opens the declarative-config PR (GitHub origin; dedicated token from its own OpenBao path, NEVER readable by SEAM) writing x-seam-deprecated.sunset + brownout into the fragment - the verdict channel IS the fragment, traveling the existing hot-reload path; caller-appears -> PR removing the block. The routes/<svc>/-only CI assertion (or self-check) fails a straying PR mechanically - the human merge gate bounds blast radius, not token scope. Zero observed traffic stays a NECESSARY condition; nothing retires autonomously.

title: Test rig: scriptable stub upstream + local OpenBao dev wiring
description: Testing Strategy -> Integration. Reusable test fixtures: (a) a scriptable stub upstream that can echo the injected credential in an error body, 401 a stale credential, return 5xx/transport faults, time out, upgrade the protocol, and emit oversized bodies - the deterministic driver for every branch of scrubbing, breaking, and self-heal; (b) local OpenBao dev-instance wiring (dev-token mode) for the login/cache/invalidation integration tests. Done when Phase 2's integration tests can script all listed behaviors.

title: 6b: Cut over kubectl-proxy fleet + delete per-cluster CLAUDE.md prose
description: Phase 6b, per-service cutover: the nine-cluster kubectl-proxy map. Checklist: ACL both ports, >=60 s retry for the worker fleet, ALL NINE per-cluster corpora green. Then delete the per-cluster read-only kubectl-proxy command prose from CLAUDE.md's Kubernetes Access section in the same change, replacing it with the SEAM /k8s/{cluster}/... pointer. NOTE: admin kubeconfig paths not fronted by SEAM stay documented; only prose SEAM actually replaces is deleted (the plan's rule: prose deleted as each fragment goes live, never ahead of it).

title: 7.5: Operator-tier gating (seam:ops:read)
description: Phase 7 (Fragment Toolchain -> who may call the control plane). /config/status, /health/credentials, /health/upstreams require seam:ops:read; refusal is a 403 NAMING the scope (deliberately not the 404 rule - these are fixed published paths); /scopes?all=1 keeps its narrower seam:scopes:read-all gate. The 1a/6a port split degrades to defense in depth behind the scope check. Verify from both ports with and without the scope.

title: Phase 11 umbrella: passive health + breaker complete
description: Phase 11 umbrella - per-origin circuit breaker under the default policy, structured 503, x-breaker tuning, three-state last-2xx render, /health/upstreams. Forcing edge 11 -> 6b (breaker live before cutover). Close ONLY when the '11' completion criteria hold (Scenario 3 green). Evidence comment required.

title: 1a.2: Serve hand-written spec - /docs, /docs/route, /openapi.json + structured 400 + version headers
description: Phase 1a (plan.md -> Implementation Phases -> Phase 1a; Architecture -> the /docs/{route} bullet). Over a hand-written whole OpenAPI spec (no fragment merge): /docs (Redoc/Swagger UI), /openapi.json, and GET /docs/route?path=<openapi-path-template>&method= returning that route's slice (params, schemas, one worked example; application/json default, text/html redirects to the /docs anchor). Request validation via pb33f libopenapi-validator; structured 400 naming the field, the expected shape, and a pointer GET /docs/route?path=...&version=_unversioned (query-parameter form ONLY - the path-segment form dies on %2F normalization). Every response carries X-SEAM-Spec-Version (spec hash) and X-SEAM-API-Version: _unversioned; ?version= accepts only _unversioned and 400s any other value naming the expected alphabet. Done when: the 1a completion-criteria rows for spec serving, structured 400, and headers pass.

title: 3.1: File-watch hot reload + atomic route-table swap
description: Phase 3 (Architecture -> reload bullet). One watcher, one descriptor PER MOUNT: each /etc/gateway/routes.d/<svc>/ plus the allowlist and CA mounts - each keyed on that mount's own ..data symlink swap (a parent-directory watch never sees a child mount's swap); on change re-merge, re-validate, atomically swap the route-table pointer; in-flight requests finish against the old immutable snapshot; zero dropped connections; sub-second. Includes the route-table-swap-under-load stress test (Testing -> Stress) under go test -race: swap while N callers stream.

title: Design the concrete route-fragment JSON Schema (x-seam-schema v1)
description:
docs/plan/plan.md Open Questions flags this as high-stakes and not yet designed:
"Exact route-fragment schema (OpenAPI fragment shape + extension fields) — not yet designed... Design once, before the first fragment."

Write a concrete JSON Schema (or Go struct + validation tags, once ADR-001's language choice lands)
covering an OpenAPI 3.1 paths/components fragment plus SEAM's extension fields, all already named in
docs/plan/plan.md's Data Models section:
- x-vault-path, x-inject-as, x-required-scope, x-credential-probe (path+method+per-fragment interval),
  x-seam-schema (version marker), x-loop-guard, x-cost-per-call/x-quota, x-upstream-map

Deliverable: a schema file (e.g. docs/notes/route-fragment-schema.json or .md with embedded schema)
that `seam lint` (Phase 9) and the gateway's runtime quarantine (Phase 1) can both validate against.
Blocks writing SEAM's first real fragment (the ArgoCD read-only proxy migration target, Phase 3).
References ADR-001 (docs/plan/plan.md) for the language the validator will eventually be written in.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Updated 2026-07-22: the field inventory + semantics are now FULLY decided in plan.md -> Data Models (schema work is mechanical, not design). Encode: all x-* fields incl. x-instance-param, x-upstream-path-template/x-upstream-strip-prefix, x-seam-owner, x-upstream-tls, x-breaker, x-fanout-scope, per-entry requiredScope; the cross-field constraints enumerated in Open Questions (both-or-neither vault-path/inject-as, quota-requires-cost + unit byte-match, window grammar, acknowledged-only fields, x-adapter mutual exclusions, brownout sanity); placement rules (x-required-scope operation-level w/ root default; x-api-version/x-seam-owner/x-seam-schema fragment-root only); leave room for x-requires-approval (approval-gated-routes note). Critical path: gates Phase 1b (bead 1b.1) and the whole graph behind it.

title: 4.2: twitterapi.io fragment + credential + e2e proof
description: Phase 4. The twitterapi.io fragment on the same terms as 4.1: credential into OpenBao under its co-owned path, x-inject-as, x-cost-per-call/x-quota in credits (byte-identical units), x-credential-probe, api.twitterapi.io as its own allowlist line, two-PR onboarding. End-to-end injection proof against the real credential. NO agent cutover.

title: 10.2: _all fan-out + envelope + caps + derived status
description: Phase 10 (Multi-Instance Routes owns status derivation; Data Models owns the envelope). _all: stages 1-9 once against the aggregate (governor draws per instance actually dispatched, projected at stage 9), stages 10-11 per surviving instance concurrently (N secrets, N injectAs, N tls policies; open-breaker instances skipped BEFORE stage 10). Envelope {instances, summary}: statuses ok/error/timeout/breaker-refused/truncated/scope-withheld with the per-status fields from Data Models; summary integers exact. maxFanoutEnvelopeBytes = min(maxBufferedResponseBytes x total, 64 MiB); completion-order admission; overflow -> truncated (httpStatus + bytes, no body), still dispatched/billed. Scrubbing ONCE over the merged artifact against the UNION of injected values (outbound stage 4, nothing after it). Derived status: 200 only if every dispatched instance 2xx and none refused/truncated/withheld; 207 + X-SEAM-Fanout-Partial: 1 otherwise; all-breaker-refused = structured 503; SEAM's own refusals (400/403/404/429/402) never appear as 207. Plus the seam lint map-width warning with the arithmetic shown.

title: 13.1: Loop breaker - canonical hash + success-reset + 429
description: Phase 13 (Per-Route Guards; Data Models -> x-loop-guard). x-loop-guard {maxRepeats, window} - window on ^[0-9]+(s|m|h|d)$, tumbling, process-anchored, restart resets unconditionally. Normalized hash preimage in order: uppercased method; matched path template + name-sorted resolved bindings; query params name-then-value sorted, percent-decoded first; canonical JSON body (RFC 8785-style: keys sorted, whitespace stripped) over the Phase 2 tee - above maxReplayableRequestBytes hash raw streamed bytes (strictly coarser, under-match only); EVERYTHING else excluded, headers wholesale. Hash taken AFTER the adapter transform (inert until Phase 8). A 2xx on the same hash clears that hash's counter - only an unbroken failing run trips. 429: plain-language body naming N repeats + re-read /docs/route pointer + Retry-After = seconds to window close + pre-Phase-7 statement that the guard is route-wide. Dry-runs and probes never counted. Key pre-7: (route, hash) - strictly more conservative; Phase 7 narrows with no fragment change. Hash property tests green (reordered keys/whitespace/query-order collapse; distinct bodies do not collide).

title: 6a.1: declarative-config manifests + first in-pod OpenBao login
description: Phase 6a (plan.md -> Phase 6a; Hosting). k8s/rs-manager/seam/: Deployment (replicas: 1, maxSurge: 0/maxUnavailable: 1, terminationGracePeriodSeconds set, env knobs incl. ports 8080/8081 + caller-facing base URL + fetch hold-down, probes wired to /_seam/healthz + /_seam/readyz on the CALLER port); ServiceAccount + projected SA-token volume; kustomization with configMapGenerator disableNameSuffixHash: true (name-stable seam-routes-<svc>); seam-upstream-allowlist at /etc/gateway/allowlist.yaml and seam-upstream-ca at /etc/gateway/upstream-ca/ as their OWN volumes (never routes.d sources); Service; Tailscale node; Lease Role/RoleBinding (coordination.k8s.io leases get/create/update, SEAM's namespace - Phase 12 consumer; NO ConfigMap API permission at all); VictoriaMetrics scrape/ServiceMonitor -> /_seam/metrics on the operator port; ArgoCD app. Prove the first in-pod Kubernetes-auth OpenBao login (dev flags refused in-cluster). Push through declarative-config; ArgoCD syncs.

title: 8.2: x-adapter - closed vocabulary + executor + lint checks
description: Phase 8 (Version Migration Strategy section 2 -> adapter resolution; Data Models -> x-adapter). Schema {targetVersion, request[], response[]} with the CLOSED vocabulary (rename/move by JSON Pointer, default a missing field, drop, wrap/unwrap envelope, rename query/header param - no expressions, no code); delegation: mutually exclusive with x-upstream/x-upstream-map and EVERY upstream-facing field (both rewrite fields, x-vault-path, x-inject-as, x-upstream-tls, x-upstream-plaintext, x-credential-probe, x-breaker) - all taken from targetVersion, so an adapter can never redirect a credential; caller-facing fields stay the old fragment's; guards charged to the target; scope requirement = union. Executor: stage 8 request transform + re-validation against the target schema (failure = 500 naming adapter, pointer, both versions - never a 400), outbound stage 2 response transform (before scrubbing; structure-altering forces the buffered path). Lint: static schema-composition check (the substantial one), targetVersion liveness, and the buffered-transform-on-unbufferable-route flag.

title: Phase 7 umbrella: per-agent scoping enforced
description: Phase 7 umbrella - stages 3+5 activate together, Grant/WhoIs enforcement, scope filtering with the 404/403 oracle rule, /whoami + /scopes, operator-tier gating, per-instance + _all scope enforcement. Blocked by the NEEDLE-side tsnet identity work (external precondition) - gateway child beads may proceed against test identities. Close ONLY when the '7' completion criteria hold with a REAL NEEDLE worker identity. Evidence comment required.

title: Phase 6a umbrella: deployed to rs-manager
description: Phase 6a umbrella - single-replica Deployment via declarative-config, first in-pod OpenBao login, probes, scrape, Lease RBAC, per-port tailnet ACL. Close ONLY when the '6a' completion criteria hold, incl. ACL verified in BOTH directions (workers reach 8080, refused on 8081). Evidence comment required.

title: Genesis: SEAM Implementation
description:
## Genesis Bead
Tied to plan: docs/plan/plan.md

## Overview
SEAM (Self-documenting Endpoint Access Mediator): single credential-injecting, self-documenting HTTP gateway fronting fleet upstreams; calling agents never see secrets. Plan v1.0, review-hardened 2026-07-22 (88% present, 0 missing). Ship order: 1a -> 1b -> 9a -> 2 -> 6a -> 3 -> 4 -> 10 -> 5 -> 11 -> 13 -> 6b -> 7 -> 12 -> 8 -> 9b -> 14. Phase numbers are stable IDs, not sequence. Every phase has explicit completion criteria (plan.md, Implementation Phases -> Phase completion criteria); the phase umbrella beads carry them as their close condition and block this genesis bead.

## Progress
- [ ] Phase 1a: Gateway scaffold
- [ ] Phase 1b: Fragment merge
- [ ] Phase 9a: seam lint CI gate
- [ ] Phase 2: Secret injection
- [ ] Phase 6a: Deploy to rs-manager
- [ ] Phase 3: ConfigMap fragments + ArgoCD pilot
- [ ] Phase 4: z.ai + twitterapi.io onboard
- [ ] Phase 10: Multi-instance + fan-out
- [ ] Phase 5: kubectl-proxy fleet (9 clusters)
- [ ] Phase 11: Passive health + circuit breaker
- [ ] Phase 13: Loop breaker + cost governor
- [ ] Phase 6b: Agent cutover (per service)
- [ ] Phase 7: Per-agent scoping (needs NEEDLE tsnet identity)
- [ ] Phase 12: Credential sentinel
- [ ] Phase 8: Version migration + retirement
- [ ] Phase 9b: seam diff / import
- [ ] Phase 14: Foreign-worker ingress

Parked (deliberately unphased, do NOT bead): approval-gated routes (docs/notes/approval-gated-routes.md). Out-of-plan backlog idea, not part of this graph: bf-16l response caching.

title: Decide loop-guard keying safety before Phase 7 identity lands
description:
docs/plan/plan.md Open Questions: before Phase 7 (per-agent tsnet identity), callers behind shared
Tailscale Connector/egress are indistinguishable, so x-loop-guard degrades to keying on
(route, request-hash) instead of (caller, route, request-hash) — per the Per-Route Guards section.

Open question: are per-route thresholds still safe to enable pre-Phase-7 with that degraded key,
or should x-loop-guard wait for identity? Concretely: with (route, hash) keying, could one caller's
legitimate repeated identical calls (e.g. a health-check poll) trip a threshold meant to catch a
different caller's runaway retry loop, and vice versa (one caller's floods masking another's)?

Write a short decision note (e.g. docs/notes/loop-guard-pre-identity.md) and fold the resolution
into docs/plan/plan.md's Per-Route Guards section. Blocks confidently enabling x-loop-guard in
Phase 13 before Phase 7 ships.
close_reason: Resolved in plan 2026-07-20: loop breaker ships in Phase 13 without waiting for identity; the degraded (route, hash) key is strictly more conservative and the 429 body states the guard is route-wide (Per-Route Guards). Implementation tracked by the Phase 13 loop-breaker bead.

title: 1b.2: Reserved-path + owner checks + merge fuzz suite
description: Phase 1b. Merge-time rejection of fragments declaring reserved control-plane paths (fragment-wide quarantine, Fragment Toolchain rule 3); x-seam-owner checks - disagreement with the mounted PARENT DIRECTORY (basename is free-form, never authoritative), or an omitted field, quarantines the whole fragment with the mismatch named at /config/status; at 1b the parent directory is simply the containing directory of the local file, proving the behavior with N files under one directory before any volume exists. Merge fuzz suite (Testing -> Property-based): malformed YAML, adversarial schemas, pathological refs, oversized docs - gateway NEVER panics, bad fragment ALWAYS quarantines whole, incumbent never evicted.

title: 9a.2: Wire seam lint as declarative-config CI gate
description: Phase 9a. Argo WorkflowTemplate (separate from seam-ci) running seam lint over k8s/rs-manager/seam/routes/** on every declarative-config PR touching that tree; failing check-classes block merge. Done when a deliberately violating PR demonstrably fails the gate for each 9a-scope check-class. This is the forcing edge 1b -> 9a -> 3: no fragment ever ships through an ungated path, including the Phase 3 pilot.

title: 14.1: Cloudflare Access ingress - JWT validation + scope map + default-deny
description: Phase 14 (Per-Agent Tool Scoping point 7's four rules, implemented verbatim). (1) SEAM validates the Access service-token JWT on EVERY request: signature vs the team domain's published JWKS, aud = SEAM's Access application ID, iss/exp/nbf - tunnel arrival proves nothing. (2) Scopes bound to the verified subject (sub/common_name) via a SEAM-side service-token->scopes map; no client-assertable scope channel exists. (3) X-SEAM-Scopes and equivalents DELETED at stage 2, not ignored. (4) Mode default-OFF; no valid JWT = 403 BEFORE route matching, secret lookup, or upstream contact - never fall through to the tailnet path. Plus the Cloudflare Tunnel + Access application setup itself. This is the ONLY phase that lifts the no-public-ingress rule. Each rule gets a named test.

title: 13.2: Cost governor + dispatch accounting + dry-run
description: Phase 13 (Per-Route Guards; Data Models). x-cost-per-call {amount, unit} / x-quota {amount, unit, window}: units byte-identical (mismatch = lint error AND merge-time quarantine), x-quota without x-cost-per-call likewise, window tumbling with lint WARNING above 168h + /config/status enumeration; SEAM converts between no units ever. Accounting: charge AT DISPATCH per upstream request actually issued - 5xx charges, the 401 refetch-retry charges twice, anything refused before dispatch (breaker 503, validation 400, loop 429, quota 402, dry-run) charges NOTHING, _all charges per dispatched instance (quota sized against map width), sentinel probes excluded. Refusal is 402 AND ONLY 402 (429 stays the loop breaker's) with Retry-After to the window's resets instant and X-SEAM-Budget-Remaining {amount=0, unit, window, resets} on the 402 itself; the header rides every quota-route response. Route-wide pool pre-Phase-7, stated as a fleet-wide fuel gauge. X-SEAM-Dry-Run: 1 = validation verdict at stage 7, no guard increment, no quota, no secret, no upstream. Scenario 6 green.

title: 8.1: Version selection (oldest default) + version-aware /docs/route
description: Phase 8 (Version Migration Strategy section 2; Data Models -> x-api-version; Phase 1a's 'what Phase 8 adds'). Selection: caller pins with X-SEAM-API-Version; ABSENT resolves to the OLDEST still-served version, never newest (the perpetually-live-worker rule); _unversioned sorts strictly oldest, addressable but never authorable; grammar ^v[1-9][0-9]*$ ordered numerically per (path, method); every response echoes the serving version. /docs/route gains: ?version= as a real selector over live versions (400 for wrong-alphabet values), canonicalVersion / deprecated / versions[] with per-entry docsUrl (incl. the _unversioned entry with isDefaultForUnversionedCallers), and every SEAM-generated pointer carrying &version= equal to the version that ACTUALLY served the request. Scenario 8's selection rows green.

title: 1a.1: Go scaffold - module, listeners, config, /_seam endpoints, container
description:
Repo currently has zero implementation code (only docs/ as of 2026-07-20) — this is the first actual
code bead for SEAM. ADR-001 in docs/plan/plan.md ratifies Go as the implementation language,
unblocking this.

Scope (Phase 1 from docs/plan/plan.md's Implementation Phases, no k8s/secrets yet — provable
standalone):
- Go module scaffold (go.mod, cmd/seam-gateway or similar)
- OpenAPI merge from a static local directory (pb33f libopenapi, per ADR-001), no ConfigMap mount yet
- /docs (rendered spec) and /openapi.json (raw merged spec)
- Request validation against the merged spec via pb33f libopenapi-validator, structured 400s naming
  the bad field, expected shape, and a /docs/{route} link (per plan.md Architecture section)
- Per-fragment runtime quarantine: a fragment that fails schema validation or collides with an
  existing path is skipped + logged, not fatal (per Fragment Toolchain section) — surfaced at
  /config/status
- X-SEAM-Spec-Version response header (hash of the merged spec, per Version Migration Strategy §C)

Depends on bd-2wt (route-fragment schema design) landing first, or at minimum a first-draft schema
to validate fixture fragments against. Does not need OpenBao, Tailscale, or K8s ConfigMaps yet —
this phase is deliberately standalone per the plan.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Correction: the schema-design dependency referenced above is bf-2wt (typo'd as bd-2wt in the description) — now recorded as a formal blocking dependency via bf dep add.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Re-scoped 2026-07-22 to Phase 1a.1 per the 1a/1b split (plan.md ADR-001 Consequences + Implementation Phases). The original description predates the split: fragment merge, collision detection, quarantine and /config/status moved to Phase 1b (beads 1b.1/1b.2, gated on bf-2wt) - do NOT build them here. 1a.1 scope: Go module per Components -> Gateway source layout (one binary 'seam', cmd/seam subcommand dispatch, internal/* packages), env/flag config (Gateway Configuration knobs incl. ports 8080/8081), both listeners bound, /_seam/healthz + /_seam/readyz on caller port, /_seam/metrics on operator port, container build. The bf-2wt dep was removed - 1a needs no fragment schema (hand-written whole spec, served by bead 1a.2). Genuinely unblocked: ready to start now.

title: 8.4: Per-route-version metric + /changes + ring buffer + archive
description: Phase 8 (Version Migration Strategy section 3; Phase 8 entry). The per-route-version request counter exported at /_seam/metrics - PROBE-FREE BY DEFINITION (origin-tag matched; a probe-inclusive counter means no probed fragment ever retires); series lands in VictoriaMetrics (6a scrape; retention now load-bearing). /changes: level 1 route list (path, verb, contract kinds added/removed/params-changed/response-changed/deprecated + visibility kinds granted/revoked, diffUrl + docsUrl - two links, not one), level 2 field diff per route; ring buffer of last N merged specs, in-memory restart-scoped; unknown/evicted since= -> 200 sinceKnown: false + pointer to the full spec, never an error. /openapi.json?version=<spec-hash> archive reads (spec-version alphabet ONLY). Serve-time scope filtering of archive + both diff levels (level-2 out-of-grant = byte-identical 404); &scope-since= + scopeChanged: unknown; archived specs NEVER served verbatim. Scope-aware pieces build on Phase 7's scope-version machinery (chain guarantees it).

title: Corpus: capture at incumbent z.ai/GLM proxy
description: Capture the differential corpus at the incumbent z.ai/GLM proxy (ardenone-cluster) BEFORE the Phase 4 fragment is written. The upstream is metered: prefer capturing from real traffic/logs over repeated synthetic replays against the metered API; keep the replay corpus small and representative.

title: NEEDLE: per-worker tsnet identity in run_process
description: Phase 7 EXTERNAL PRECONDITION (Per-Agent Tool Scoping point 4; Proof Obligations Ledger row 6). In ~/NEEDLE (Rust): run_process (src/dispatch/mod.rs) currently mints NO identity for spawned workers - all workers collapse into one indistinguishable WhoIs caller behind shared egress. Deliver: per-worker ephemeral tsnet-embedded Tailscale node, tagged (tag:needle-worker), provisioned at dispatch and injected via the existing adapter.environment passthrough or a new template variable; workers become first-class tailnet peers SEAM can WhoIs individually. Different repo - coordinate with the NEEDLE workspace; keep this deliverable SEAM-independent (no SEAM code needed). Blocks the Phase 7 umbrella; SEAM-side 7.x beads may proceed in parallel against test identities.

title: 2.4: Upstream TLS policy + acknowledged exceptions
description: Phase 2 (Architecture -> upstream TLS). Verify against the system trust store with hostname checking by default; NO global skip-verify switch. x-upstream-tls: caBundle resolves to /etc/gateway/upstream-ca/<key> (manifest-owned ConfigMap mount; local dev --upstream-ca-dir, refused in-cluster, enumerated as upstreamCaSource: dev-dir) - absent/unparseable key QUARANTINES the fragment, never silently falls back to system trust; serverName SNI override; insecureSkipVerify accepts only 'acknowledged'. x-upstream-plaintext: acknowledged required on any http:// x-upstream (fragment-level; the per-entry map form ships with Phase 10). /config/status enumerates every route on skip-verify or plaintext, and every dev-mode source.

title: Phase 2 umbrella: secret injection complete
description: Phase 2 umbrella - OpenBao client + cache + outage behavior, dual allowlists, strip-then-inject, path rewrite, TLS policy, body tee, scrubbing. The heaviest early phase. Close ONLY when the '2' completion criteria hold (incl. Scenario 1 end-to-end and scrub property tests at every offset). Evidence comment required.

title: 2.5: Inbound request-body tee (maxReplayableRequestBytes)
description: Phase 2 (Credential Health Sentinel specifies the mechanism; assigned to Phase 2 - edges 2 -> 13 and 2 -> 12). Buffer the inbound body up to maxReplayableRequestBytes (default 1 MiB, INDEPENDENT knob - shares only a default with maxBufferedResponseBytes, tuning one never moves the other); tee upstream while streaming; discard when the response status is known; above the cap or unreplayable (request-side protocol upgrade, unbounded chunked with no declared length) the body streams through unbuffered and each consumer degrades per its own section. Build the buffer ONLY - the 401 retry is Phase 12's, the loop-hash canonicalization Phase 13's.

title: 2.6: Secret-echo scrubbing + property tests + log-capture test
description: Phase 2 (Credential Health Sentinel -> scrubbing; ships WITH injection, not with the sentinel). Buffered path (known CL <= maxBufferedResponseBytes: decompress, scan, redact to [REDACTED-BY-SEAM], re-encode); incremental path (chunked/event-stream/oversized-known-CL: len(secret)-1 carry-over overlap, longest secret where several are injected) - the cap is hold-whole, never a scrubbability gate; headers AND trailers scanned on the same terms; unscannable responses (protocol upgrades, opaque binary, undecodable encodings) REFUSE injection absent x-unscrubbable: acknowledged (lint-flagged, /config/status-enumerated). Tests: scrub property/fuzz at every byte offset and chunk size (a single missed boundary is a leaked credential), AND the log-capture test (Security Threat Matrix -> Audit logging): run the acceptance suite at max verbosity, grep every sink for each injected value, a hit fails the build.

title: Phase 10 umbrella: multi-instance + fan-out complete
description: Phase 10 umbrella - x-instance-param + x-upstream-map resolution, instance-segment deletion, _all fan-out envelope with caps/truncation and derived status. Must precede Phase 5 (forcing edge 10 -> 5). Close ONLY when the '10' completion criteria hold (Scenario 5 pre-Phase-7 form green). Evidence comment required.

title: Phase 12 umbrella: credential sentinel live
description: Phase 12 umbrella - Lease-elected probe loop, /health/credentials per (fragment, instance), 401 refetch-retry-once + credential-refresh-not-retried envelope, probe exclusions. Close ONLY when the '12' completion criteria hold (Scenario 4 both forms green; exclusions verified). Evidence comment required.

title: 1b.1: Fragment parse + merge + collision triple + quarantine
description: Phase 1b (plan.md). Fragment parsing including the FULL x-* extension-field inventory per the bf-2wt schema (Data Models is the authority); OpenAPI merge from a static local directory; collision detection on the (path, method, x-api-version) triple - _unversioned-beside-v1 is a legal coexistence pair, only an exact triple match collides, the later-merged file loses (deterministic filename order) and the incumbent is never evicted; quarantine + /config/status (loaded fragments, versions, quarantined ones with error text). Gated on bf-2wt. Done when: the 1b completion-criteria merge/collision/coexistence rows pass.

title: Phase 1b umbrella: fragment merge complete
description: Phase 1b umbrella - fragment merge, collision triple, quarantine, owner checks, fuzz suite. Close ONLY when the '1b' completion criteria (plan.md, Phase completion criteria) hold on a shipped commit. Claiming = verification + evidence comment; children carry the build.

title: 7.3: Scope filtering - the 404/403 oracle rule
description: Phase 7 (Architecture -> scope filtering under /docs/route; Version Migration Strategy paragraph on level-2 /changes). Filter applied BEFORE the existence check on /openapi.json, /docs, /docs/route, /changes: a route outside the caller's filtered spec returns 404 BYTE-IDENTICAL to a route that never existed (body names /whoami and the caller's X-SEAM-Scope-Version; SEAM logs the resolved route server-side); the scope-naming 403 (missing scope + the Grant snippet that fixes it) ONLY for visible-but-not-invocable (right path, wrong method); a map route visible if the caller holds scope for at least one instance's effective requirement. Guard refusals (429/402/503) are never dressed as authorization. Scenario 7 green including the byte-identity check (body, headers, timing).

title: 7.4: /whoami + /scopes + X-SEAM-Scope-Version + bounded map
description: Phase 7 (Per-Agent Tool Scoping points 5-6). /whoami: resolved identity, tags, effective scopes, current X-SEAM-Scope-Version. /scopes: derived at request time from TWO merged sources - the merged spec's x-required-scope declarations inverted to scope->routes, plus the builtin control-plane set compiled into the binary (labeled source: builtin) - scope-filtered by default; /scopes?all=1 behind seam:scopes:read-all. X-SEAM-Scope-Version (hash of the caller's effective scope set) on every response. The bounded hash->scope-set retention map: most recent 4 distinct scope versions per identity, 24 h idle eviction, global LRU cap - the BOUND is the deliverable; current-scope filtering never reads this map.

title: Precondition: provision OpenBao role + policy for SEAM
description: Precondition of Phase 2 (Implementation Phases -> dependency edges; NOT a 6a deliverable). In the active OpenBao (rs-manager instance): create the Kubernetes-auth role seam bound to SEAM's future ServiceAccount + namespace, and the policy granting read on seam/routes/* and NOTHING else - the hostile-fragment threat model rests on that being literally true. Server side only; 6a keeps the cluster half (SA object, projected token volume, first in-pod login proof). Done when a token obtained via that role can read a test secret under seam/routes/ and is denied everything outside it.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Fenced from autonomous workers (status=blocked, 2026-07-22): OpenBao provisioning needs operator credentials and admin judgment. Unblock deliberately or do interactively from ex44.

title: Decide fan-out (_all) partial-failure semantics for multi-instance routes
description:
docs/plan/plan.md Open Questions (and Multi-Instance Routes and Fan-Out section) leaves this undecided:
when the reserved `_all` instance fans a request out to every mapped upstream (x-upstream-map) and
some instances error or time out, what does SEAM return?

Three candidates named in the plan, none yet chosen:
1. Fail-closed — any instance error fails the whole request
2. Fail-open 200 — per-instance error entries plus a hard-to-miss summary count
3. 207-style multi-status

Write a short decision note (docs/notes/fan-out-partial-failure.md, following the pattern of
docs/notes/quiet-window-options.md) comparing the three against SEAM's actual use case (e.g.
`/k8s/_all/api/v1/pods?fieldSelector=status.phase!=Running` fleet-wide triage queries), pick one,
and fold the decision into docs/plan/plan.md's Multi-Instance Routes and Fan-Out section the same
way other 2026-07-16 decisions were folded in. Blocks Phase 10.
close_reason: Resolved in plan 2026-07-20: 207 Multi-Status with per-instance result entries; fail-closed rejected (Multi-Instance Routes and Fan-Out; envelope in Data Models). Implementation tracked by the Phase 10 fan-out bead.

title: 9a.1: seam lint CLI - 9a-scope check set
description: Phase 9a (plan.md -> Phase 9a full check list). Package the gateway's own merge/validate engine as seam lint (same binary, subcommand - Components -> Gateway source layout: internal/spec shared). Checks that land AT 9a: fragment schema; triple collisions; reserved control-plane paths; owner-chain links visible pre-6a (declarative-config directory name -> x-seam-owner -> x-vault-path prefix; treat the not-yet-existing SEAM manifest and allowlist as INERT, never as violations); x-api-version grammar ^v[1-9][0-9]*$ + rejection of AUTHORED _unversioned; transport-acknowledgement hard errors and flags; IP-literal rejection + well-formed-absolute-URL checks. Later checks land WITH their phases (2: path-rewrite coherence; 10: map-width warning; 11: x-breaker conflicts; 13: cost coherence; 8: deprecated/brownout/adapter checks) - those live in those beads, do not add them here.

title: Corpus: capture at incumbent ArgoCD read-only proxy
description: Testing Strategy: 'the corpus is captured at the incumbent proxy' BEFORE the service's fragment is written. Capture representative real request/response pairs at the ArgoCD read-only proxy (the argocd-ro endpoint agents use today), covering the routes agents actually call (applications list, app status, clusters). Blocks the Phase 3 pilot fragment bead.

title: Phase 9b umbrella: authoring convenience complete
description: Phase 9b umbrella - seam diff + seam import --from-url. Genuinely deferrable; 9a makes authoring safe, this makes it pleasant. Close ONLY when the '9b' completion criteria hold. Evidence comment required.

title: Export existing internal health/guard signals as Prometheus metrics
description:
Not yet in docs/plan/plan.md. SEAM's design already accumulates a rich set of internal signals that
are currently only pull-per-agent (an agent has to call the endpoint to see them):
- /health/credentials (Credential Health Sentinel section)
- /health/upstreams + per-upstream circuit breaker state (Passive Route Health section)
- last-2xx per route (Passive Route Health section)
- X-SEAM-Budget-Remaining per caller (Per-Route Guards / Cost Governor section)
- per-route-version request-count (Version Migration Strategy §B, used for usage-gated retirement)

None of this is exported as Prometheus metrics, so it's invisible to existing fleet dashboards
(FABRIC on lab, ~/FABRIC) unless something scrapes SEAM's JSON endpoints specially. Add a /metrics
endpoint (standard Prometheus exposition format) surfacing these as counters/gauges, so SEAM's health
shows up next to NEEDLE worker telemetry in the existing observability surface instead of living only
behind agent-facing JSON endpoints.

Write it up as a short addition to docs/plan/plan.md (a couple sentences under Passive Route Health
or its own small subsection) noting it's additive to the existing per-signal endpoints, not a
replacement — /health/credentials etc. stay as the agent-facing structured views. Natural fit: land
alongside Phase 11 (Passive Route Health) since that's when the underlying data starts existing.
close_reason: Superseded by plan: /_seam/metrics is now a Phase 1a endpoint on the operator port, and the per-route-version series + guard/breaker/quarantine/cache counters are Phase 8 deliverables scraped by VictoriaMetrics. Covered by the 1a scaffold and Phase 8 metric beads.

title: 12.1: Sentinel probe loop + Lease + /health/credentials + exclusions
description: Phase 12 (Credential Health Sentinel). Background loop issuing each fragment's x-credential-probe (per-fragment cadence, per-entry probeInterval override) as a byte-identical authenticated request carrying an IN-PROCESS origin tag (never a wire header - all X-SEAM-* are stripped on ingress, so the tag is unforgeable). Lease-elected: acquire the Kubernetes Lease before probing, drop on SIGTERM; without the Lease serve traffic but probe NOTHING (fail closed; consumes the 6a Role/RoleBinding, adds no RBAC). /health/credentials per (fragment, instance) (_default for single-instance): lastVerified, status, known expiry, per-instance probe-spend rate - never values. Exclusions per consumer: OUT of the retirement counter, loop breaker, and quota; IN last-2xx and breaker state with source: probe labeling. Deprecated fragments keep probing; adapting fragments never probe; probe on a pass-through is a lint error.

title: 11.1: Per-origin circuit breaker + x-breaker + structured 503
description: Phase 11 (Passive Route Health owns the policy). Failure = transport faults (refused/reset, DNS, TLS handshake), timeouts, and 5xx ONLY - no 4xx ever counts, and a 401 NEVER counts (even one triggering refetch-retry); SEAM's own 400/429/500 never count. 5 consecutive -> open 30 s; half-open admits ONE real caller (no synthetic probe), any non-failure closes, re-failure re-opens with backoff 30/60/120 s capped at 5 min; keyed on resolved upstream origin (scheme+host+port; per x-upstream-map entry). Structured 503: upstream, openedAt, lastError, derived Retry-After (seconds remaining, floored at 1, body matches header). x-breaker {threshold, openSeconds, maxOpenSeconds, enabled} fragment-root + per-instance override; same-origin disagreement: lint fails the PR, runtime strictest values govern with the disagreement at /config/status; ON BY DEFAULT - enabled: false is the lint-flagged, enumerated opt-out. Scenario 3 green.

title: Phase 1a umbrella: gateway scaffold complete
description: Phase 1a umbrella - Go scaffold, two listeners, hand-written spec serving, structured 400s, control-plane branch, /_seam endpoints. Close ONLY when the '1a' entry under plan.md -> Implementation Phases -> Phase completion criteria holds on a shipped commit (same-commit DoD floor: gofmt/vet/golangci-lint clean, go test -race green). Claiming this bead = run the verification end to end and record evidence in a comment; the child beads carry the build. Do not close on belief.

title: 7.2: Stage-5 authorization + per-instance/_all scope enforcement
description: Phase 7 (Architecture stage 5; Data Models -> x-upstream-map requiredScope, x-fanout-scope). Stage-5 enforcement: x-required-scope as conjunctive scope-set, default-deny; effective requirement = union of operation-level, adapter-target (inert until Phase 8 exists), and selected entry requiredScope; _all additionally gated on fragment-root x-fanout-scope, with unscoped instances DROPPED at the stage-9/10 boundary as scope-withheld envelope entries (no dispatch, no secret, no charge - never a whole-request 403 for a partially-scoped caller). Control-plane branch step 3 becomes real: builtin scope declarations (seam:ops:read, seam:scopes:read-all) evaluated, refusal = 403 naming the scope. Scenario 5's scope-withheld case green.

title: Phase 5 umbrella: kubectl-proxy fleet onboarded
description: Phase 5 umbrella - nine-cluster x-upstream-map fragment, six new Connectors, eight bare-MagicDNS allowlist lines, per-instance credentials + requiredScope. Close ONLY when the '5' completion criteria hold (every one of the nine instances succeeds through SEAM; _all returns nine labeled entries). Evidence comment required.

title: Phase 9a umbrella: seam lint CI gate live
description: Phase 9a umbrella - seam lint CLI + declarative-config CI gate, live BEFORE the first fragment ships (edge 1b -> 9a -> 3). Close ONLY when the '9a' completion criteria hold: gate demonstrably fails a violating PR per check-class, treats absent SEAM manifest/allowlist as inert. Evidence comment required.

title: 7.1: Stage-3 identity resolution live (WhoIs/Grant) + tailnet Grants
description: Phase 7 (Architecture -> stages 3/5 activation; Per-Agent Tool Scoping points 1-2). Stage 3 goes live: WhoIs on the inbound connection, Grant app-capability scope claims parsed, default-deny a request whose identity cannot be resolved; the reserved anonymous identity retires. Write the tailnet policy Grant entries carrying scope claims for the worker tags. HARD RULE: stages 3 and 5 activate TOGETHER and never separately (deny without check refuses everyone; check without identity evaluates nobody) - this bead pairs with 7.2 and the pair lands as one deployment.

title: Corpus: capture at incumbent twitterapi.io proxy
description: Capture the differential corpus at the incumbent twitterapi.io proxy (twitterapi-proxy-svc on ardenone-cluster) BEFORE the Phase 4 fragment is written. Metered upstream (per-call credits): capture from real traffic where possible; keep replays minimal and representative.

title: 5.1: Six Tailscale Connectors on rs-manager
description: Phase 5 infra (Hosting -> Phase 5 target set) - no SEAM code dependency, workable early. Add Connectors on rs-manager via declarative-config, following the three existing ones, for: apexalgo-iad, iad-options, iad-kalshi, iad-native-ads, iad-ci, ord-devimprint. (ardenone-cluster and ardenone-manager already reachable; rs-manager itself needs none - in-cluster Service DNS; ardenone-hub decommissioned, excluded.) Done when each cluster's kubectl-proxy bare-MagicDNS host answers from rs-manager.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Fenced from autonomous workers (status=blocked, 2026-07-22): Tailscale Connector rollout touches cluster networking and needs tailnet admin verification. Do via declarative-config interactively; unblock deliberately.

title: Phase 8 umbrella: version migration + retirement complete
description: Phase 8 umbrella - version selection (oldest default), adapters, Deprecation/Sunset + brownout, per-route-version metric, /changes + ring buffer, retirement evaluator. Close ONLY when the '8' completion criteria hold (Scenario 8 green; evaluator opens a correctly-scoped PR against a drained test route). Evidence comment required.

title: 3.2: ArgoCD pilot pass-through fragment + corpus green
description: Phase 3. Author and ship the pilot: the existing hand-rolled ArgoCD read-only proxy as a PASS-THROUGH fragment (declares neither x-vault-path nor x-inject-as - the proxy injects its own read-only token server-side; legal per Data Models; scrubbing no-op, no /health/credentials entry, x-credential-probe would be a lint error). Files at k8s/rs-manager/seam/routes/<svc>/ in declarative-config, through the live 9a lint gate; onboarding = two-PR operation (volume+volumeMount in SEAM's Deployment - a rollout - plus the fragment; host covered by the *.ardenone.com suffix); x-upstream-strip-prefix per the worked example. Then run the differential corpus green - meeting its own 6b precondition early. First real fragment through the whole path.

title: Corpus: capture at the nine incumbent kubectl-proxies
description: Capture per-cluster differential corpora at the nine incumbent kubectl-proxies (the read-only observer endpoints in CLAUDE.md's per-cluster commands) BEFORE the Phase 5 fragment is written. Representative GETs per cluster (pods, deployments, events, namespaces). One corpus per cluster; the byte-identical API should make these near-uniform.

title: 9b.1: seam diff + seam import --from-url
description: Phase 9b (Fragment Toolchain). seam diff: renders the effective merged-spec change a declarative-config PR would cause, for review. seam import --from-url: bootstraps a fragment from an upstream's own published OpenAPI spec (ArgoCD and Kubernetes both publish one), filtered to selected paths/verbs; injection metadata (x-vault-path etc.) curated by hand afterwards. Done when a real PR's diff renders correctly and an imported+curated ArgoCD fragment passes seam lint.
